title: U.S. and Israel Intensify Airstrikes on Tehran and Isfahan
detail: U.S. and Israeli forces executed a broad-scale wave of strikes targeting infrastructure in Tehran and Isfahan, marking the most aggressive phase in the weeklong conflict. The attacks aimed at undermining the Iranian regime's facilities and military capabilities.

media.caption: U.S. and Israel Intensify Airstrikes on Tehran and Isfahan

title: Iran Launches Missile Strikes on Israel
detail: In retaliation to intensified airstrikes, Iran fired missiles targeting Israel. The Israeli military confirmed the attacks and reported that air defense systems intercepted several missiles, preventing significant damage.

media.caption: Iran Launches Missile Strikes on Israel

title: U.S. Approves $151 Million Arms Sale to Israel
detail: The U.S. government approved a $151 million arms deal with Israel to bolster its military capabilities amid the ongoing conflict with Iran. The deal includes advanced munitions and defense systems.

media.caption: U.S. Approves $151 Million Arms Sale to Israel

title: Iranian President Apologizes to Neighboring Countries
detail: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian issued a rare apology to neighboring countries for recent attacks, signaling a desire to de-escalate regional tensions. However, hard-line leaders within Iran criticized this stance and indicated continued retaliation against U.S. and Israeli targets.

media.caption: Iranian President Apologizes to Neighboring Countries

title: U.S. Warns of Intensified Bombing Campaign
detail: The U.S. administration warned of an impending and intensified bombing campaign against Iran, marking the most aggressive phase in the ongoing conflict. President Trump demanded Iran's unconditional surrender before any negotiations.

media.caption: U.S. Warns of Intensified Bombing Campaign

title: Iranian Missile Strikes Target Gulf States
detail: Iran launched missile and drone attacks on several Gulf countries, including Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Bahrain, and the United Arab Emirates. Saudi officials reported intercepting multiple drones heading toward the Shaybah oil field.

title: U.S. Suspends Operations at Kuwait Embassy
detail: The United States announced the suspension of its embassy operations in Kuwait amid escalating regional tensions and security concerns stemming from the ongoing conflict with Iran.

media.caption: U.S. Suspends Operations at Kuwait Embassy

title: Israel Claims Destruction of Over 60% of Iran's Missile Launchers
detail: Israel's military chief reported that more than 60% of Iran's ballistic missile launchers and 80% of its air-defense systems have been destroyed in the ongoing U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran.

media.caption: Israel Claims Destruction of Over 60% of Iran's Missile Launchers

title: Iran's Ballistic Missile Stockpile Depleting
detail: Reports indicate that Iran's ballistic missile stockpile is depleting rapidly due to the ongoing conflict, with estimates suggesting that Iran may have only around one thousand ballistic missiles remaining.

title: Iranian Strike on Oil Tanker in Strait of Hormuz
detail: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) attacked the Malta-flagged oil tanker Prima with a drone after the vessel allegedly ignored repeated warnings from the IRGC Navy regarding the prohibition of traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.

media.caption: Iranian Strike on Oil Tanker in Strait of Hormuz

title: Israeli Strikes Kill 12 in Lebanon; Oil Facility in Tehran Targeted
detail: Israeli airstrikes on southern Lebanon and Beirut resulted in 12 fatalities, targeting commanders of Iran’s Revolutionary Guards’ Quds Force. Additionally, an Israeli strike on an oil storage facility in Tehran caused significant fires, marking the first targeting of a civilian industrial site in the conflict.

media.caption: Israeli Strikes Kill 12 in Lebanon; Oil Facility in Tehran Targeted

title: U.S. and Israel Consider Deploying Special Forces to Secure Iran's Nuclear Stockpile
detail: The U.S. and Israel are discussing the deployment of special forces to Iran to secure its stockpile of highly enriched uranium, aiming to prevent the development of nuclear weapons. Iran possesses approximately 450 kilograms of 60%-enriched uranium, which could be converted into weapons-grade material within weeks.

title: Iran Rejects Cease-Fire Negotiations, Prepares for U.S. Ground Invasion
detail: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi rejected the idea of a cease-fire and declared the country's readiness to confront a U.S. ground invasion. This follows statements from U.S. President Donald Trump, who did not rule out deploying American troops.

media.caption: Iran Rejects Cease-Fire Negotiations, Prepares for U.S. Ground Invasion

title: Explosions Rock Tehran; U.S. Warns of Intensified Bombing
detail: A series of explosions occurred in Tehran as the U.S. warned of an impending intensified bombing campaign. The Trump administration authorized a $151 million arms deal with Israel, with President Trump demanding Iran’s 'unconditional surrender' before any negotiations.

media.caption: Explosions Rock Tehran; U.S. Warns of Intensified Bombing

title: U.S. and Israel Attacking Iran at 'Record Pace', War Monitor Says
detail: Research group Airwars reported that the U.S. and Israel have attacked Iran at a pace far surpassing other recent conflicts, with about 4,000 targets struck in the first four days of the offensive.

media.caption: U.S. and Israel Attacking Iran at 'Record Pace', War Monitor Says

title: Israeli Airstrikes on Tehran Fuel Depots Cause Toxic Smoke and Acid Rain
detail: Israeli airstrikes targeted multiple fuel depots in Tehran, resulting in prolonged fires that released hazardous fumes. The Iranian capital is now engulfed in toxic smoke, leading to black acid rain and severe air pollution. Residents report 'unbreathable' air, with many experiencing respiratory issues and skin irritation. Iran's Red Crescent has warned that the rain is highly acidic and poses significant health risks.

media.caption: Smoke rising over Tehran following Israeli airstrikes on fuel depots.

title: Mojtaba Khamenei Named Successor to Iran's Supreme Leader
detail: Iranian state television announced that Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, has been appointed as his successor. This move consolidates control over military strategy through the Revolutionary Guard amid the ongoing conflict. The appointment follows the death of the elder Khamenei in an Israeli strike, which has escalated tensions in the region.

media.caption: Mojtaba Khamenei Named Successor to Iran's Supreme Leader

title: U.S. Weighs Deploying Special Forces to Secure Iran's Nuclear Stockpile
detail: The United States, in coordination with Israel, is considering deploying special forces into Iran to secure its stockpile of highly enriched uranium. Concerns center around Iran's 450 kilograms of uranium enriched to 60%, which can be rapidly converted to weapons-grade material. This mission would require ground forces to penetrate heavily fortified nuclear storage sites.

media.caption: U.S. Weighs Deploying Special Forces to Secure Iran's Nuclear Stockpile

title: U.S. Expresses Dismay Over Israel's Strikes on Iranian Fuel Depots
detail: The United States expressed dismay over Israel's airstrikes on 30 Iranian fuel depots, carried out without prior consultation. While Israel notified the U.S. military in advance, American officials were surprised by the scale of the operation. The strikes caused massive fires visible across Tehran and filled the capital with smoke. U.S. officials are concerned that targeting infrastructure used by civilians could rally Iranian public opinion behind the regime and destabilize global oil markets.

media.caption: U.S. Expresses Dismay Over Israel's Strikes on Iranian Fuel Depots

title: Iran Launches Missile and Drone Attacks on Gulf Countries
detail: Iran launched 16 ballistic missiles and over 117 drones targeting Gulf countries, including the United Arab Emirates and Bahrain. The UAE's Defense Ministry reported intercepting all 16 missiles and most of the drones, though four drones fell in UAE territory. Bahrain reported damage to a desalination plant from an Iranian drone strike. These attacks are part of Iran's retaliation against U.S. and Israeli strikes.

media.caption: Iran Launches Missile and Drone Attacks on Gulf Countries

title: Iran Launches Missiles at Israel; No Casualties Reported
detail: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) claimed responsibility for missile strikes targeting military sites in Tel Aviv and Beersheba, as well as the Muwaffaq Salti Air Base in Azraq, Jordan. Israeli rescue services reported at least three people injured by shrapnel from the missiles. The attacks are part of Iran's ongoing retaliation against U.S. and Israeli military actions.

media.caption: Iran Launches Missiles at Israel; No Casualties Reported

title: Iranian Drone Strike Sets Kuwait City Tower Ablaze
detail: An Iranian drone strike targeted a tower belonging to the Public Institution for Social Security in Kuwait City, causing a significant fire. The attack is part of Iran's broader campaign of retaliatory strikes against U.S. allies in the region. Kuwaiti authorities reported two officers killed during the incident.

media.caption: Iranian Drone Strike Sets Kuwait City Tower Ablaze
